fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but deeply unprofitable with a high valuation and significant cash burn.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by a wide margin (-0.66 vs -0.20); next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Gross margin 44.8% — healthy unit economics on current sales.
Total cash $3.03B and current ratio 18.47 — strong liquidity position.
Forward P/E -386.14 indicates expected future profitability, though current losses are extreme.
negatives: Operating margin -1014.0% and Profit margin 0.0% — significant operational losses.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-1.41B — substantial cash burn rate.
Price/Sales (TTM) 524.30 — extremely high valuation relative to current sales.
Last quarter EPS surprise of -233.7% indicates significant earnings miss.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile shares plummeted significantly, leading other space stocks lower, following a reported explosion of a Blue Origin rocket. This incident overshadowed recent positive performance, with the company having been the best-performing communication services stock over the past month. The sharp decline suggests the market is reacting negatively to perceived risks in the broader space launch sector, impacting ASTS despite its prior momentum.
